title: Triangles
subject: math
audioScript: Good morning, everyone. Today we're opening Triangles. Forget the heavy textbook tone for a moment — think of this chapter as a story about Similar triangles, Basic Proportionality Theorem, and Pythagoras theorem. I'll use everyday examples first, then we'll connect them to what CBSE expects in the exam.

segments.teleprompter: Good morning, everyone. Today we're opening Triangles. Forget the heavy textbook tone for a moment — think of this chapter as a story about Similar triangles, Basic Proportionality Theorem, and Pythagoras theorem. I'll use everyday examples first, then we'll connect them to what CBSE expects in the exam.

Let's lock in the must-know words for Triangles. Don't memorise long paragraphs — learn what each term means in one simple sentence. If you can explain these words to a friend, you're already halfway there.
In this part — core concepts — we'll keep it conversational. Chapter: Triangles. I'll connect the idea to something you've seen in daily life before we go exam-deep.
Formulas can feel scary, but they're just shortcuts. For Triangles, I'll say what each symbol means in plain language, then show when to use it — not just how to copy it from the board.
In this part — solved examples — we'll keep it conversational. Chapter: Triangles. I'll connect the idea to something you've seen in daily life before we go exam-deep.
In this part — common mistakes — we'll keep it conversational. Chapter: Triangles. I'll connect the idea to something you've seen in daily life before we go exam-deep.

Look at the diagram on screen. Trace it with your finger while I talk. For Triangles, the picture is worth more than a page of notes — notice labels, direction arrows, and what changes from left to right.

Time to practise — not to panic. I've lined up about 9 questions on Triangles, covering Similar triangles, Basic Proportionality Theorem, and Pythagoras theorem. Cover your notes, attempt each question in exam order, then check against the sections above.

Let's connect Triangles to real life — not just the textbook. I'll walk through everyday examples for Similar triangles, Basic Proportionality Theorem, and Pythagoras theorem: kitchen, lab, transport, or things you see in your neighbourhood.

Before you press play on any video about Triangles, grab a notebook. Pause every two minutes and write one sentence in your own words. The links here are curated for Class 10 CBSE — NCERT-style explainers and one-shot revision.

This is your recap if you watched a video on Triangles — or if you prefer reading first. I'll walk through the lesson flow: Similar triangles, Basic Proportionality Theorem, and Pythagoras theorem, one common mistake, and what you should be able to explain without notes afterward.
Imagine I'm sitting beside you before the board exam. Here's Triangles the way I'd explain it to a student who said 'I read it but didn't get it.' Slow pace, simple words, one idea at a time.
Quick recap of Triangles. Three things to remember tonight: the big idea, one formula or rule, and one mistake to avoid. That's enough for a confident revision.

sections.title: 8. Practice Questions
sections.body:
_Class 10 CBSE practice for **Triangles**._

**Additional practice set**

- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Similar triangles** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Triangles.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Similar triangles** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Basic Proportionality Theorem** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Triangles.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Basic Proportionality Theorem** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Pythagoras theorem** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Triangles.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Pythagoras theorem** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **NUMERICAL (3 marks)** — Using **AB/DE = BC/EF = AC/DF**, solve a standard board-style problem from Triangles. Show all working steps.
- **NUMERICAL (3 marks)** — Using **AB^2 + BC^2 = AC^2**, solve a standard board-style problem from Triangles. Show all working steps.
- **LONG (5 marks)** — Long answer: Describe the main ideas of **Triangles**. Cover at least three syllabus topics and one real-life application.

sections.title: 11. Video Transcript Summary
sections.body:
_Teacher recap — what a strong Class 10 video lesson on **Triangles** should cover:_

**Lesson flow (as if narrated on screen):**

1. **Hook** — Start with a daily-life example tied to Triangles, not a textbook definition.
2. **Similar triangles** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
3. **Basic Proportionality Theorem** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
4. **Pythagoras theorem** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
5. **Formulas** — Write AB/DE = BC/EF = AC/DF on the board, explain each symbol, then solve one board-style numerical.
6. **Common mistake** — Warn about the error students make most often in exams.
7. **Recap** — Three bullet points to remember before moving to practice questions.

**After watching, you should be able to:**

- Explain Similar triangles in everyday language.
- Draw or describe one diagram related to Triangles.
- Attempt at least two short-answer questions without notes.

**Videos linked for this chapter:**

- 🔍 [CBSE class 10 Triangles NCERT explained Hindi English](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=CBSE+class+10+Triangles+NCERT+explained+Hindi+English)
- 🔍 [Triangles class 10 one shot revision](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=Triangles+class+10+one+shot+revision)
- 🔍 [NCERT Mathematics class 10 Triangles animation](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=NCERT+Mathematics+class+10+Triangles+animation)
